method_atom: A large-N asymptotic analysis of an ensemble of many mutually-repelling points whose joint correlations have determinantal structure generated by a reproducing kernel that is scaled up by a large integer parameter. As the parameter grows the macroscopic point density converges in probability to an equilibrium measure characterized by a constrained variational (weighted-energy) problem governed by a fully nonlinear Monge-Ampere-type operator; global fluctuations of linear statistics (even of merely Lipschitz test functions) are asymptotically Gaussian and described by a log-correlated Gaussian free field; and the microscopic rescaled correlations converge to a universal limiting kernel independent of local details.
object_atom: Determinantal point processes / free fermions living on a compact complex manifold, whose correlation kernels are the Bergman kernels of a high tensor power of a Hermitian holomorphic line bundle, with concrete instances being normal random matrix ensembles, the two-dimensional Coulomb gas, free fermions in a strong magnetic field, and multivariate orthogonal polynomials.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the determinantal-ensemble machinery with its equilibrium-measure / Monge-Ampere variational limit, Gaussian-free-field fluctuations, and universal microscopic scaling; the domain-specific part is the complex-geometric realization via Bergman kernels of line-bundle powers on a complex manifold (and its physical avatars).
